items.original_title: SDL_GLContext broken on windows that have not at least once been set visible
items.original_body:
# This bug report was migrated from our old Bugzilla tracker.

These attachments are available in the static archive:

* [simple program to illustrate bug behavior (main.cpp, text/plain, 2011-01-20 14:03:51 +0000, 739 bytes)](https://bugzilla.libsdl.org/attachment.cgi?id=554)
* [Possible fix (possible-fix.patch, text/plain, 2012-07-01 09:31:46 +0000, 939 bytes)](https://bugzilla.libsdl.org/attachment.cgi?id=892)

**Reported in version:** HG 2.0
**Reported for operating system, platform:** Linux, x86_64

# Comments on the original bug report:

On 2011-01-20 14:01:48 +0000, Matthias wrote:

> Please see attached file. 
> 
> The program should create a (hidden) window, create an opengl context, then clear the color buffer of that context, wait and exit.
> 
> Unless the created window is set visible at least once before calling SDL_GL_CreateContext, the result is that the window is shown, but the gl context doesn't seem to work (i.e. opengl function calls have no effect).
> 
> If the window is set to visible at least once (or shown, and subsequently hidden again), then, if the window is shown again after context generation, opengl calls work as intended.
> 
> This issue observed on Ubuntu Linux 10.04, 2.6.32-27-generic kernel, NVIDIA 195.36.24 driver. SDL head changeset was 5054:8af42aba8e17

On 2011-01-20 14:03:51 +0000, Matthias wrote:

> Created attachment 554
> simple program to illustrate bug behavior
> 
> There is a function showAndHide() in the main program. If you remove the comments, the program works as intended. If you do not remove the comments, the bug appears (i.e. broken SDL_GLContext).

On 2012-07-01 09:31:46 +0000, driedfruit wrote:

> Created attachment 892
> Possible fix
> 
> Please try the attached patch as a possible fix. I don't think it's related directly to hide/show, but to a general race condition.

On 2013-05-21 01:27:20 +0000, Sam Lantinga wrote:

> Hey Ryan, can you look at this for SDL 2.0 release? You can probably recruit Pierre-Loup if you want.

On 2013-07-11 11:59:36 +0000, Ryan C. Gordon wrote:

> 
> This is working here. I think we probably fixed it when we did all the tapdancing with fullscreen awhile ago, since it forced us to wait on some window mapping event or whatever, unrelated to this.
> 
> The specific reason this was failing, if I had to guess, was this section of the glXMakeCurrent docs:
> 
> "The first time ctx is made current to any thread, its viewport is set to the full size of drawable. Subsequent calls by any thread to glXMakeCurrent with ctx have no effect on its viewport."
> 
> Probably before the window was mapped, it had a 0x0 size.
> 
> If you're still having this problem with the latest SDL2 code, please see if adding "glViewport(0, 0, winw, winh);" right after the SDL_GL_CreateContext() fixes it...it's possible you had a 0x0 viewport.
> 
> (And if that fixes it, maybe we should force that in SDL_GL_CreateContext().)
> 
> Alternately: if you're still having this problem, see if SDL_GL_CreateContext() returned NULL. The reasons are still roughly the same, but that could also be why it failed, and we'd like to know where to fix this.
> 
> --ryan.

On 2013-07-11 13:39:46 +0000, Sam Lantinga wrote:

> Ryan, I'm going to mark this waiting for feedback from Matthias so we get the info you're requesting.

On 2013-07-12 18:52:57 +0000, Ryan C. Gordon wrote:

> 
> (Sorry if you get a lot of copies of this email, we're touching dozens of bug reports right now.)
> 
> Tagging a bunch of bugs as target-2.0.0, Priority 1.
> 
> This means we're in the final stretch for an official SDL 2.0.0 release! These are the bugs we really want to fix before shipping if humanly possible.
> 
> That being said, we don't promise to fix them because of this tag, we just want to make sure we don't forget to deal with them before we bless a final 2.0.0 release, and generally be organized about what we're aiming to ship.
> 
> Hopefully you'll hear more about this bug soon. If you have more information (including "this got fixed at some point, nevermind"), we would love to have you come add more information to the bug report when you have a moment.
> 
> Thanks!
> --ryan.

On 2013-07-30 00:28:31 +0000, Ryan C. Gordon wrote:

> 
> I'm resolving this bug, as it appears to be fixed. If you find it's still broken on the latest version of SDL, please feel free to reopen this bug.
> 
> --ryan.

items.original_title: Filter out duplicates in the GUI
items.original_body: By default, the GUI shows all screenshots, including both the PNG and JPG versions of the same screenshot.\r\n\r\nFiltering out the PNG files from the screenshot GUI that have JPEGs available under the same name would fix this.
items.mutated_title: Filter duplicates in the GUI. By default, the GUI shows all screenshots, including both the PNG and JPG versions of the same screenshot. Filtering the PNG files from the screenshot GUI that have JPEGs available under the same name would fix this.
items.mutated_body: By default, the GUI shows all screenshots, including both the PNG and JPG versions of the same screenshot.\r\n\r\nFiltering out the PNG files from the screenshot GUI that have JPEGs available under the same name would fix this.

items.original_body:
# This bug report was migrated from our old Bugzilla tracker.

**Reported in version:** don't know
**Reported for operating system, platform:** All, All

# Comments on the original bug report:

On 2007-07-31 16:04:01 +0000, Kenney Phillis Jr wrote:

> Pretty much add a few new functions that help with listing/getting directory paths.
> 
> //Translators for Pre Mac OSX
> char* sdl_win32tomac(char* file);
> char* sdl_unixtomac(char* file);
> // unix path conversion.
> char* sdl_mactoUnix(char* file);
> char* sdl_win32tounix(char* file);
> // win32 path conversion.
> char* sdl_UnixtoWin32(char* file);
> char* sdl_MacToWin32(char* file);
> 
> //file listing functions.
> char** sdl_listfiles(char* directory); // list of all files in a directory.
> char** sdl_listfiles(char* directory, char* ext); // list of all files in a directory of a specified extension.
> char** sdl_listdirectories(char* directory);// list directories in specified path.

On 2009-09-20 22:51:52 +0000, Sam Lantinga wrote:

> We'll keep this in mind for SDL 1.3, thanks!

> We'll keep this in mind for SDL 1.3, thanks!

items.original_title: [QUESTION] The generated ./_timing.json file is not JSON, but newline-separated stringified JSON. Why?
items.original_body:
# What / Why

Our org had a bit of a strange issue pop up today; in our CI we run a basic check that any JSON file in our build (that is hypothetically from us) is valid JSON, which usually prevents errant trailing commas or the lack thereof from breaking things anywhere that requires() or parses a resource/configuration/messaging/etc. file, which has saved much time/heartache. A new developer was looking into caching to speed up builds and so had generated one with `npm ci --cache .npm --timing`, which then failed at our JSON validation step because, as described in the title, `.npm/_timing.json` just gets stringified JSON appended to it, which makes the entire file invalid JSON as soon as there are 2 entries or more. 

Since he was also experimenting with moving the cache to the working directory, we weren't (previously) ignoring `.npm/` like we were for e.g. `node_modules/`. Obviously, we're not going to consider `.npm/` files part of our build and can take steps on our end to ignore them, but it seemed strange that something with the ".json" extension wouldn't actually be a JSON file. Have you thought about using a different extension?

## Where

* Anytime you run `npm` with `--timing`, as documented (and more than one entry is generated, e.g.

```
{"version":"6.14.5","command":["debug","--timing"],"logfile":"/path/to/.npm/_logs/2020-05-21T21_21_38_564Z-debug.log"} 
{"version":"6.14.5","command":["--timing","i"],"logfile":"/path/to/.npm/_logs/2020-05-21T21_22_24_221Z-debug.log"}
```
)

## Who

* Anyone who runs this?

## References

Supposing you would change this to ".jsons" or something, a cursory glance says that the references would need to be updated:

* https://github.com/npm/cli/blob/83293c4f805d670708bbf7c24a95f517b7b4cc24/lib/utils/error-handler.js#L42
* https://github.com/npm/cli/blob/cc3122a2fe4619615bf87860b49272ed5ac30335/docs/content/using-npm/config.md#L1187
* hypothetically any tooling that interacts with this file that I'm not privy to.

items.original_title: Provide the Api doc for older SDK versions
items.original_body:
Hi

My azure function is using azure-cosmo 3.1.2 .
I cannot find the api doc anymore. I can only find the latest version.
Can you provide the Api doc for older SDK versions as well?
If I don't have the doc, I'll need to refactor all my azure functions whenever you publish a new version.

Thanks

items.original_title: Add a color picker in the "Add shared calendars" dialog
items.original_body:
#### Current behaviour 

There is no way to select the color for a shared calendar.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/24670327/101612488-46ca2100-3a3d-11eb-9249-e573d42084cc.png)

#### Suggested behaviour

- [ ] There should be a "Color" column next to the "Rights" column to allow users to select the color of a shared calendar.
- [ ] The colors will also be randomly selected by default.
- [ ] Make sure it displays properly in mobile devices.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/24670327/101614233-4894e400-3a3f-11eb-98d1-11b7d57ea9ab.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/24670327/101614430-81cd5400-3a3f-11eb-8b72-ab89933d22d4.png)

items.original_body:
Hi 

it would be great to allow some special chars. For example I would like to allow '/' and tried to extend slugify with:
```
slugify.extend({'/':'/'})
```
without success. Any idea how to solve this?

items.original_title: CLI crashes when the config file is missing
items.original_body: Running the command without a config file raises an uncaught exception instead of showing a helpful error.
items.mutated_title: How do I install the CLI on Windows?
items.mutated_body: I am new to this project and would like to know the correct installation steps for Windows.
